channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Teen accused of setting alight a Cairns childcare centre granted bail
item.description: A magistrate has told the court a "strong case couldn't be confidently asserted" against a teen accused of setting fire to a Cairns early learning centre.
item.dc:creator: Rosanne Maloney

media:group.media:description: The early learning centre in Bentley Park was allegedly set on fire and sustained significant damage.

item.title: Man charged after allegedly making threats at north Brisbane mosque
item.description: Masjid Taqwa's board said the alleged incident caused fear and distress among members of the congregation.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Richards

media:group.media:description: A man has been charged after allegedly threatening people at the Masjid Taqwa mosque in Bald Hills.

item.title: Breaking: Jury finds accused guilty of love triangle chase and murder
item.description: Three years ago, Dale Aaron McKenna was pursued down a dark country road at 180km/h, dying when his vehicle rolled. Now, the man chasing him has been convicted of his murder.
item.dc:creator: Cindy Wockner and Grace Nakamura

media:group.media:description: Clinton Gordon Sutcliffe pleaded not guilty to the murder of Dale Aaron McKenna.

item.title: Guilty plea for man who murdered 'respected' island council worker
item.description: Four and a half years after a respected council worker was murdered by his housemate, his killer has been sentenced.
item.dc:creator: Chloe Chomicki and Baz Ruddick

media:group.media:description: Günther Franco Messmer's family shared this photo and a statement with the ABC.

item.title: Dua Lipa sues Samsung for using her face to sell TVs
item.description: The lawsuit alleges Samsung featured a copyrighted image of the pop star on the front of cardboard boxes containing televisions for sale.

media:group.media:description: Dua Lipa is seeking at least $15 million in damages from the tech giant.

item.title: Alleged Russian spies to face fresh espionage conspiracy charge
item.description: Australian citizens Kira and Igor Korolev were charged in 2024, accused of attempting to gather and send state secrets to Russia.
item.dc:creator: Tara Cassidy

media:group.media:description: Kira and Igor Korolev have been accused of attempting to gather and send state secrets to Russia.

item.title: 'Crying all the time': University academic got through cancer treatment but not her teaching workload
item.description: The University of Newcastle has been found to be worst in class when it comes to staff wellbeing, now an academic with terminal cancer is speaking out about it because others fear they cannot.
item.dc:creator: Conor Duffy and Emily Jane Smith

media:group.media:description: Associate Professor Trisha Pender has worked at the University of Newcastle for nearly two decades.

item.title: Fewer cars on Red Centre roads as drive market dries up amid rising fuel prices
item.description: People in central Australia's tourism industry say drive tourism is down, with some wayside inns around Alice Springs reporting about a 75 per cent drop in overnight stays.
item.dc:creator: Victoria Ellis, Stewart Brash, Oliver Lee-Young, and Charles Geary

media:group.media:description: Janine and Marc Colwell are being frugal on their trip north.

item.title: Breaking: Boy rushed to hospital after being hit by train at Melbourne station
item.description: The ABC understands a boy, believed to be 11 or 12 years old, was hit and trapped under a carriage for some time.

media:group.media:description: Emergency services were called to the North Melbourne Railway Station about 4pm on Monday.

item.title: Body found near Lake Cargelligo believed to be alleged triple killer
item.description: A body has been found near Lake Cargelligo, where three people were shot and killed in January this year, sparking a massive manhunt.
item.dc:creator: Xanthe Gregory, Bruce MacKenzie, and Ethan Rix

media:group.media:description: Suspected triple-murderer Julian Ingram was on the run since the fatal shootings in late January.

item.title: 'Premature': One Nation's chances in Sydney's west played down
item.description: One Nation's Barnaby Joyce says potential candidates for Western Sydney were first and second-generation Australians, but an analyst calls him a "great media performer".

media:group.media:description: Barnaby Joyce's claim that One Nation could sweep seats in Westen Sydney have been hosed down.

item.title: Drink, drug driver jailed for 11 years over motorcyclist death
item.description: A County Court judge has sentenced a drink, drug driver to 11 years' jail after the man hit and killed a motorcyclist while behind the wheel of a stolen ute in Central Victoria.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Mangelsdorf

media:group.media:description: David Graham Berry has been sentenced to 11 years' jail — a higher than standard sentence.

item.title: Crows footballer tells fans to 'choose kindness' after social media abuse
item.description: Adelaide Crows footballer Josh Rachele has called out fan behaviour after receiving an abusive message online amid an ongoing conversation on mental health in the AFL.
item.dc:creator: Rachael Merritt

media:group.media:description: 23-year-old Josh Rachele has responded to a comment sent on social media.

item.title: Hantavirus-hit cruise ship passengers to quarantine in Perth
item.description: Australian passengers who were on board the hantavirus-hit cruise ship will be ordered to quarantine in Perth for at least three weeks.
item.dc:creator: Caitlyn Gribbin

media:group.media:description: Multiple people have died while on the hantavirus-hit ship, carrying 88 passengers and 61 crew.

item.title: $100m bid to reduce Canberra to Sydney train travel time to under 4 hours
item.description: The federal, ACT and NSW governments will spend $100 million upgrading the Canberra to Sydney rail link in a bid to reduce the train trip to under four hours.
item.dc:creator: Penny Travers

media:group.media:description: It currently takes more than four hours to travel from Canberra to Sydney via train.

item.title: Aboriginal graffiti artist remorseful for sacred site desecration
item.description: An Aboriginal man has pleaded guilty to vandalising a culturally significant site in Victoria's Alpine National Park.
item.dc:creator: Philippe Perez

media:group.media:description: The vandalism at Paradise Falls occured in early May 2025.

item.title: Alleged bashing murder of mother by son 'likely fuelled by illicit drug use' court hears
item.description: Crafers West man Bo Sebastian De-Simone, 40, is standing trial in South Australia's Supreme Court, charged with the murder of his mother Linda Simon, 62, in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Eva Blandis

media:group.media:description: Bo Sebastian De-Simone is standing trial accused of murdering his mother, Linda Simon, in 2022.

item.title: NT Police say pepper spraying Network 10 camera operator 'not inappropriate'
item.description: Northern Territory Police have reviewed an incident in which an officer pepper sprayed a Network 10 camera operator during rioting in Alice Springs last month, concluding his actions were "not inappropriate".
item.dc:creator: Matt Garrick and Elsie Lange

media:group.media:description: Footage captured by the ABC shows an NT Police officer pepper spraying Matthew Bonser during the riots.

item.title: Some universities regain access to Canvas as hacker's deadline looms
item.description: Australian universities and education departments have begun regaining access to online learning platform Canvas, after being caught up in a breach last week. However, the deadline set by the hackers continues to count down.
item.dc:creator: Monty Jacka

media:group.media:description: Hundreds of thousands of students were last week caught up in an incident of cybercrime relating to online learning management system Canvas.

item.title: Mother found not guilty of fatally poisoning son with spiked smoothie
item.description: After hearing evidence from more than 60 witnesses across five weeks, a jury retired to deliberate on Thursday afternoon and returned a verdict on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Talissa Siganto

media:group.media:description: Maree Crabtree (centre) denied killing her son Jonathan.

item.title: Good behaviour bond given to NDIS worker for fraudulent claims to keep provider afloat
item.description: An Adelaide man has avoided jail for making a series of fraudulent claims with the NDIS in a bid to help the struggling business he was working for survive.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Mason

media:group.media:description: Adrian Bembrick leaves Adelaide District Court after being spared jail for defrauding the NDIS.

item.title: Social housing demand jumps 500pc in city where baby died in riverside camp
item.description: A report by advocacy group Homelessness NSW shows demand for priority social housing has more than doubled across the state in the four years to June 2025, with a 500 per cent increase in Wagga Wagga.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Pezet

media:group.media:description: Around a dozen tents had been set up at a camp on the Murrumbidgee River.

item.title: Indulgent breakfast recipes to enjoy at home
item.description: From shakshuka to French toast, you can enjoy an indulgent breakfast or brunch at home with one of these recipes.
item.dc:creator: Anna Chisholm

media:group.media:description: This flavourful shakshuka with crumbled feta is a satisfying breakfast choice.

item.title: Hasrat's life changed the day she stumbled across the T20 World Cup
item.description: State and WBBL cricketer Hasrat Gill has risen through the ranks of youth Australian cricket. She is one of many South Asian teenagers flocking to the sport.
item.dc:creator: Marnie Vinall

media:group.media:description: Indian-born Australian Hasrat Gill has represented Australia in the Under-19 Women’s squad.

item.title: First Nations man dies in Queensland prison
item.description: The death is not being treated as suspicious and a report is being prepared for the coroner. Fourteen Indigenous people have died in Australian prisons this year.
item.dc:creator: Bridget Herrmann

media:group.media:description: The prisoner died at Lotus Glen Correctional Centre.
